sections.label: Section 158
sections.original: ἐπώνυμος μὲν κάρτα καὶ φιλόπτολις
sections.translation: True to his name indeed , his country ’ s friend is Dolon . Your father ’ s house was famed before ,

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: νῦν δὶς τόσως ἔθηκας εὐκλεέστερον .
sections.translation: but now you have made it doubly so .

sections.label: Section 161
sections.original: οὐκοῦν πονεῖν μὲν χρή , πονοῦντα δʼ ἄξιον
sections.translation: So must I toil , but for my pains I should receive fitting wages . For set a reward on any deed , and it breeds a double favor .

sections.label: Section 164
sections.original: ναί , καὶ δίκαια ταῦτα κοὐκ ἄλλως λέγω .
sections.translation: Yes , that is fair ; I cannot dispute it .

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: τάξαι δὲ μισθόν , πλὴν ἐμῆς τυραννίδος .
sections.translation: Name your wage , except for my sovereignty .

sections.label: Section 166
sections.original: οὐ σῆς ἐρῶμεν πολιόχου τυραννίδος .
sections.translation: I do not covet your toilsome sovereignty .

sections.label: Section 167
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἀλλὰ γήμας Πριαμιδῶν γαμβρὸς γενοῦ .
sections.translation: Well then , marry a daughter of Priam and become my brother-in-law .

sections.label: Section 168
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἐξ ἐμαυτοῦ μειζόνων γαμεῖν θέλω .
sections.translation: No , I do not wish to marry among those beyond my station .

sections.label: Section 169
sections.original: χρυσὸς πάρεστιν , εἰ τόδʼ αἰτήσεις γέρας .
sections.translation: There ’ s gold , if this you ’ ll claim as your prize .

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔστʼ ἐν οἴκοις · οὐ βίου σπανίζομεν .
sections.translation: I have it in my home ; I lack no sustenance .

sections.label: Section 171
sections.original: τί δῆτα χρῄζεις ὧν κέκευθεν Ἴλιος ;
sections.translation: What then is your desire of all that Ilium stores within her ?

sections.label: Section 172
sections.original: ἑλὼν Ἀχαιοὺς δῶρά μοι ξυναίνεσον .
sections.translation: Promise me my gift when you conquer the Achaeans .

sections.label: Section 173
sections.original: δώσω · σὺ δʼ αἴτει πλὴν στρατηλάτας νεῶν .
sections.translation: I will give it to you ; ask anything except the captains of the fleet .

sections.label: Section 174
sections.original: κτεῖνʼ , οὔ σʼ ἀπαιτῶ Μενέλεω σχέσθαι χέρα .
sections.translation: Slay them ; I do not ask you to keep your hand off Menelaus .

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: οὐ μὴν τὸν Ἰλέως παῖδά μʼ ἐξαιτῇ λαβεῖν ;
sections.translation: Is it the son of Oileus you would ask me for ?

sections.label: Section 176
sections.original: κακαὶ γεωργεῖν χεῖρες εὖ τεθραμμέναι .
sections.translation: Hands that are well brought up are worthless at farming .

sections.label: Section 177
sections.original: τίνʼ οὖν Ἀχαιῶν ζῶντʼ ἀποινᾶσθαι θέλεις ;
sections.translation: Whom then of the Achaeans will you have alive to hold to ransom ?

sections.label: Section 178
sections.original: καὶ πρόσθεν εἶπον · ἔστι χρυσὸς ἐν δόμοις .
sections.translation: I told you before , my house is stored with gold .

sections.label: Section 179
sections.original: καὶ μὴν λαφύρων γʼ αὐτὸς αἱρήσῃ παρών .
sections.translation: Why then , you shall come and with your own hands choose out some spoil .

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: θεοῖσιν αὐτὰ πασσάλευε πρὸς δόμοις .
sections.translation: Nail up the spoils for the gods on their temples .

sections.label: Section 181
sections.original: τί δῆτα μεῖζον τῶνδέ μʼ αἰτήσεις γέρας ;
sections.translation: Then what greater prize than these will you ask me for ?

sections.label: Section 182
sections.original: ἵππους Ἀχιλλέως · χρὴ δʼ ἐπʼ ἀξίοις πονεῖν
sections.translation: Achilles ’ horses . The prize must be worth the toil when one stakes one ’ s life on Fortune ’ s dice .

sections.label: Section 184
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐρῶντί γʼ ἀντερᾷς ἵππων ἐμοί ·
sections.translation: Ah ! but your desires clash with mine about those horses ;

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: ἐξ ἀφθίτων γὰρ ἄφθιτοι πεφυκότες
sections.translation: for they are immortal and born from immortals , who bear the son of Peleus on his headlong course . Poseidon , lord of the ocean , broke them and gave them to Peleus , so runs the legend . Yet , for I urged you on , I will not break my word ; I will give to you

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: κάλλιστον οἴκοις κτῆμʼ , Ἀχιλλέως ὄχον .
sections.translation: Achilles ’ team , a fair possession for your house .

sections.label: Section 191
sections.original: αἰνῶ · λαβὼν δʼ ἄν φημι κάλλιστον Φρυγῶν
sections.translation: I thank you ; in receiving then , I assert that I am taking a fairer gift than any other Phrygian for my bravery . Yet you should not be envious ; you have other things to gladden your heart , in your kingship over this land .

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: μέγας ἀγών , μεγάλα δʼ ἐπινοεῖς ἑλεῖν ·
sections.translation: Great the enterprise , and great the reward you design to receive . Happy , yes , happy will you be , if you succeed ; fair the fame your toil shall win . Yet it is a great thing to become the brother-in-law of princes . On the gods ’ decrees let Justice keep her eye !

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: τὰ δὲ παρʼ ἀνδράσιν τέλειά σοι φαίνεται .
sections.translation: what man can give you have , it seems , in full .

sections.label: Section 201
sections.original: στείχοιμʼ ἄν · ἐλθὼν δʼ ἐς δόμους ἐφέστιος
sections.translation: I will set forth ; but going within my house I will clothe myself in fitting attire , and then I will hasten to the Argive fleet .

sections.label: Section 204
sections.original: ἐπεὶ τίνʼ ἄλλην ἀντὶ τῆσδʼ ἕξεις στολήν ;
sections.translation: Why , what dress in place of this will you assume ?

sections.label: Section 205
sections.original: πρέπουσαν ἔργῳ κλωπικοῖς τε βήμασι .
sections.translation: One that fits my task and furtive steps .

sections.label: Section 206
sections.original: σοφοῦ παρʼ ἀνδρὸς χρὴ σοφόν τι μανθάνειν ·
sections.translation: One should ever learn wisdom from the wise ; tell me , what will be your equipment ?

sections.label: Section 208
sections.original: λύκειον ἀμφὶ νῶτʼ ἐνάψομαι δορὰν
sections.translation: I will fasten a wolf-skin about my back , and over my head put the brute ’ s gaping jaws ;

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: βάσιν τε χερσὶ προσθίαν καθαρμόσας
sections.translation: then fitting its fore-feet to ny hands and its hind-feet to my legs , I will go on all-fours in imitation of a wolf ’ s gait to puzzle the enemy , when I approach their trenches and barriers round the ships . Rut whenever I come to a deserted spot ,

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: δίβαμος εἶμι · τῇδε σύγκειται δόλος .
sections.translation: I will walk on two feet ; such is the ruse I have decided on .

sections.label: Section 216
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὖ σʼ ὁ Μαίας παῖς ἐκεῖσε καὶ πάλιν
sections.translation: May Hermes , Maia ’ s child , escort you safely there and back , prince of tricksters as he is ! You know what you have to do ; good luck is all you need now .

sections.label: Section 219
sections.original: σωθήσομαί γε καὶ κτανὼν Ὀδυσσέως
sections.translation: I shall return in safety , and bring to you the head of Odysseus

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: οἴσω κάρα σοι — σύμβολον δʼ ἔχων σαφὲς
sections.translation: when I have slain him , or the son of Tydeus , and with this clear proof before you you shall assert that Dolon went to the Argive fleet ; for , before the dawn , I will come back home with bloodstained hand . Exit Dolon .

sections.label: Section 1.224
sections.original: Θυμβραῖε καὶ Δάλιε καὶ Λυκίας
sections.translation: Lord of Thymbra and of Delos , who haunt

sections.label: Section 1.225
sections.original: ναὸν ἐμβατεύων
sections.translation: your temple in Lycia , Apollo , O divine head , come with all your archery , appear this night , and by your guidance save this man ,

sections.label: Section 1.230
sections.original: ἁγεμὼν καὶ ξύλλαβε Δαρδανίδαις ,
sections.translation: and aid the Dardanians , O almighty god whose hands in days of old built the walls of Troy .

sections.label: Section 1.233
sections.original: μόλοι δὲ ναυκλήρια , καὶ στρατιᾶς
sections.translation: May he come to the ships ! May he reach the army of Hellas and spy it out , then turn again and reach the altars of his father ’ s

sections.label: Section 1.235
sections.original: ἵκοιτο , καὶ κάμψειε πάλιν θυμέλας οἴ-
sections.translation: home in Ilium !

sections.label: Section 1.237
sections.original: Φθιάδων δʼ ἵππων ποτʼ ἐπʼ ἄντυγα βαίη ,
sections.translation: May he mount the chariot drawn by Phthia ’ s horses , when our master has sacked Achaea ’ s camp ,

sections.label: Section 1.240
sections.original: τὰς πόντιος Αἰακίδᾳ
sections.translation: those horses that the sea-god gave to Peleus , son of Aeacus .

sections.label: Section 2.245
sections.original: λήματος · ἦ σπανία τις
sections.translation: his spirit ; how few stout hearts there are , when on the sea the sunlight dies and the city labors in the surge .

sections.label: Section 2.250
sections.original: πόλις . ἔστι Φρυγῶν τις ἔστιν ἄλκιμος ·
sections.translation: Phrygia yet has left a valiant few , and bold hearts in the battle ’ s press ; it is only Mysia ’ s sons who scorn us as allies .

sections.label: Section 2.254
sections.original: τίνʼ ἄνδρʼ Ἀχαιῶν ὁ πεδοστιβὴς σφαγεὺς
sections.translation: Which of the Achaeans will the earth-treading murderer slay

sections.label: Section 2.255
sections.original: οὐτάσει ἐν κλισίαις , τετράπουν
sections.translation: in their beds , as he pretends to be a four-footed beast on the ground ? May he lay Menelaus low , slay Agamemnon and bring his head

sections.label: Section 2.260
sections.original: Ἑλένᾳ κακόγαμβρον ἐς χέρας γόον ,
sections.translation: to Helen ’ s hands , causing her to lament her evil kinsman , who has come against my city , against the land of Troy with his army of a thousand ships .

sections.label: Section 264
sections.original: ἄναξ , τοιούτων δεσπόταισιν ἄγγελος
sections.translation: Lord , in days to come may it be mine

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: εἴην τὸ λοιπὸν οἷά σοι φέρω μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: to bring my masters such news as I am bearing to you now .

sections.label: Section 266
sections.original: ἦ πόλλʼ ἀγρώταις σκαιὰ πρόσκειται φρενί ·
sections.translation: Often the rustic mind is afflicted with dullness ; so you have probably come to this ill-suited place to tell your master , in armor , about the sheep ! Do you not know my palace or my father ’ s throne ,

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: οἷ χρῆν γεγωνεῖν σʼ εὐτυχοῦντα ποίμνια ;
sections.translation: where you should carry your tale when you have prospered with your flocks ?

sections.label: Section 271
sections.original: σκαιοὶ βοτῆρές ἐσμεν · οὐκ ἄλλως λέγω .
sections.translation: Dull we herdsmen are ; I do not dispute it . But none the less I bring joyful news to you .

sections.label: Section 273
sections.original: παῦσαι λέγων μοι τὰς προσαυλείους τύχας ·
sections.translation: Cease your tale of how the sheep-fold fares ; I have battles to fight and spears to wield .

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: τοιαῦτα κἀγὼ σημανῶν ἐλήλυθα ·
sections.translation: The very things of which I , too , came to tell you ; for a chieftain of a countless army is on his way to join you as your friend and ally of this land .

sections.label: Section 278
sections.original: ποίας πατρῴας γῆς ἐρημώσας πέδον ;
sections.translation: His country ? and the home that he has left ?

sections.label: Section 279
sections.original: Θρῄκης · πατρὸς δὲ Στρυμόνος κικλήσκεται .
sections.translation: Thrace ; men call his father Strymon .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: Ῥῆσον τιθέντʼ ἔλεξας ἐν Τροίᾳ πόδα ;
sections.translation: Did you say that Rhesus was setting foot in Troy ?
